steps.title: Lock in the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two paintings. The correct choice must point to something the paintings share, supported by details from the notes.

steps.title: Find the shared feature in the notes
steps.content: Both paintings are described as huge, and the listed dimensions (149 × 255 inches and 132 × 264 inches) are very close in scale. That is a concrete shared trait the notes support.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Monkman... 2019; Leutze... 1851': contrasts artists and dates, so it highlights differences, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the people and actions the two paintings portray are very different': explicitly emphasizes a difference. ✗
  • Choice C — 'both huge, measuring 149 × 255 inches and 132 × 264 inches': uses "both" and matching dimensions to spotlight a shared trait. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Revolutionary War soldiers, while... Indigenous people and refugees': the "while" construction frames a contrast, not a similarity. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
